parts.content: How do I restart a pod?

messages.instructions:

content.title: Kubernetes v1.35: New level of efficiency with in-place Pod restart
content.author: [Yuan Wang](https://github.com/yuanwang04) [Giuseppe Tinti Tomio](https://github.com/GiuseppeTT) [Sergey Kanzhelev](https://github.com/SergeyKanzhelev)
content.chunk:
## Observing the restart

To make this process observable, a new Pod condition, `AllContainersRestarting`, is added to the Pod's status. When a restart is triggered, this condition becomes `True` and it reverts to `False` once all containers have terminated and the Pod is ready to start its lifecycle anew. This provides a clear signal to users and other cluster components about the Pod's state.

All containers restarted by this action will have their restart count incremented in the container status.

content.title: kubectl rollout restart
content.chunk:
## {{% heading "examples" %}}

```
  # Restart all deployments in the test-namespace namespace
  kubectl rollout restart deployment -n test-namespace
  
  # Restart a deployment
  kubectl rollout restart deployment/nginx
  
  # Restart a daemon set
  kubectl rollout restart daemonset/abc
  
  # Restart deployments with the app=nginx label
  kubectl rollout restart deployment --selector=app=nginx
```

content.title: Kubernetes v1.35: 通过就地重启 Pod 实现更高的效率
content.author: [Yuan Wang](https://github.com/yuanwang04) [Giuseppe Tinti Tomio](https://github.com/GiuseppeTT) [Sergey Kanzhelev](https://github.com/SergeyKanzhelev)
content.chunk:
## Introducing the `RestartAllContainers` action

To address this, Kubernetes v1.35 adds a new action to the container restart rules: `RestartAllContainers`. When a container exits in a way that matches a rule with this action, the kubelet initiates a fast, **in-place** restart of the Pod.
-->

content.title: Kubernetes v1.34: Finer-Grained Control Over Container Restarts
content.author: [Yuan Wang](https://github.com/yuanwang04)
content.chunk:
## Learn more

- Read the documentation for
  [container restart policy](/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle/#container-restart-rules).
- Read the KEP for the
  [Container Restart Rules](https://github.com/kubernetes/enhancements/tree/master/keps/sig-node/5307-container-restart-policy)

content.title: Pod 的生命周期
content.chunk:
## Pod lifetime

Whilst a Pod is running, the kubelet is able to restart containers to handle some
kind of faults. Within a Pod, Kubernetes tracks different container
[states](#container-states) and determines what action to take to make the Pod
healthy again.
-->

parts.content:
You can restart a pod using the `kubectl rollout restart` command. For example, to restart all deployments in a namespace, you can use the command `kubectl rollout restart deployment -n test-namespace`. To restart a specific deployment, you can use `kubectl rollout restart deployment/nginx`. You can also restart daemon sets using `kubectl rollout restart daemonset/abc`. Additionally, you can use the `--selector` option to restart deployments with a specific label, such as `kubectl rollout restart deployment --selector=app=nginx`. 

For more information, you can refer to the [Kubernetes documentation](https://github.com/kubernetes/website/blob/main/content/en/docs/reference/kubectl/generated/kubectl_rollout/kubectl_rollout_restart.md). 

Please note that the `kubectl rollout restart` command is used to restart resources, and the specific command may vary depending on the resource you want to restart. 

Also, you can refer to the [Kubernetes v1.35 documentation](https://github.com/kubernetes/website/blob/main/content/en/blog/_posts/2026/restart-all-containers.md) for more information on restarting containers and pods. 

It's also worth noting that the `RestartAllContainers` action is available in Kubernetes v1.35, which allows for a fast, in-place restart of a pod when a container exits. You can refer to the [Kubernetes v1.35 documentation](https://github.com/kubernetes/website/blob/main/content/en/blog/_posts/2026/restart-all-containers.md) for more information on this feature. 

Lastly, you can refer to the [Pod lifecycle documentation](https://github.com/kubernetes/website/blob/main/content/zh-cn/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/pod-lifecycle.md) for more information on the different states of a pod and how Kubernetes handles restarting containers.
